name: domain-crispr-overview
description: Use web_search to search for 'CRISPR gene editing clinical trials progress 2025 2026'. Summarize the current state of CRISPR clinical trials in 3 bullet points. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
• As of early 2026, over 250 gene-editing clinical trials are tracked globally, with 150+ active trials spanning CRISPR-Cas9, base editing, prime editing, and epigenetic editing across blood disorders, cardiovascular disease, cancer, and rare genetic conditions.
• Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel), the first approved CRISPR therapy for sickle cell disease and beta-thalassemia, is now approved in 8+ markets; Intellia and Beam have advanced in vivo and base-editing candidates into Phase III trials.
• Key 2025 milestones include Cleveland Clinic's first-in-human CRISPR cholesterol-lowering trial, the first personalized LNP-delivered CRISPR therapy for an infant, and Beam's first clinical correction of a disease-causing mutation via base editing.
key_facts: Over 250 gene-editing clinical trials are being tracked globally, with 150+ currently active as of 2025-2026
Casgevy is approved in 8+ markets including the US, UK, EU, and several Middle Eastern countries
Cleveland Clinic's Phase 1 trial of CTX310 demonstrated safe reduction of LDL cholesterol and triglycerides via CRISPR
Beam Therapeutics achieved the first-ever clinical correction of a disease-causing mutation using base editing
The first personalized in vivo CRISPR therapy was delivered to an infant via lipid nanoparticles in 2025
